rubric.evidence: Headless-Chrome full flow (fresh profile): first visit shows loader state=download-required with a Download button (NO auto-download); after Download, state=ready; the C-major-scale sample traced to 'Median pitch 391.5 Hz — G4. 218 of 240 frames voiced' in 1225 ms with backend 'wasm (onnxruntime-web)'. Console + network clean. Standalone runnability probe (onnxruntime-web 1.20.1, crepe_onnx_tiny.onnx): a 440 Hz sine → 440.42 Hz (conf 0.93); batched multi-octave test 110/220/440/880/261.63 Hz all recovered within ~1 Hz. Screens: reports/responsive/screens/pitch-detection/desktop.png, mobile.png.
rubric.notes: CREPE has NO transformers.js pipeline; inference is driven with onnxruntime-web directly, isolated to models/pitch-detection/worker.js (silero-vad precedent). Shared lib/webai.js untouched. Whisper (multi-model) uses the shared lib/webai.js loader.

rubric.evidence: Output is a per-window 360-bin pitch salience [N×360]; the f0 is the confidence-weighted centre of the peak (cents→Hz, marl/crepe mapping) and the peak height is the voicing confidence. The page renders the real salience pitchgram + the extracted f0 on a musical-note grid + a per-note histogram. On the scale sample the histogram shows the scale notes evenly (D4/E4/F4/A4/B4/C5 ~12–13% each); on the glide/vibrato samples the contour tracks continuously; on the JFK speech clip (multi-model) median 228 Hz (A♯3), range 60–708 Hz, 55% voiced — plausible male-speech prosody.
rubric.notes: pitch-estimation is a new task (registered in models.json + a conformance-lib IO_SHAPE template; index.html renders it dynamically).

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (niobures/CREPE, linked), task (pitch-estimation), runtime (onnxruntime-web), dtype (fp32), and backend (WASM). Inference runs off the main thread in a Web Worker (models/pitch-detection/worker.js), batched (128 frames/run). Conformance runner: declares-model-id / declares-task / declares-backend / declares-quantisation / inference-off-main-thread all auto-checkable and passing.

Uses the SHARED createModelLoader auto-init architecture. The worker stores the ~1.9 MB ONNX under its real HF URL (contains /niobures/CREPE/) in Cache Storage, so lib/model-cache.js detects it and auto-initialises on return. Fresh-profile flow confirmed download-required→ready with no silent download; conformance shared-auto-init-loader = pass. The multi-model page runs two shared loaders (CREPE + Whisper), both auto-init from cache.

Loader announces checking → downloading (progress bar wired to the worker's byte-progress) → ready via role=status aria-live. Failures surface on the page (.status.err) with a Retry path. Backend is WASM only (no WebGPU requirement) so it runs everywhere; no blank/faked state. Console + network clean across first visit and the multi-model two-model download.

Every control exercised headlessly across the 5 pages: sample chips (aria-pressed), file dropzone (role=button, Enter/Space), mic record buttons, confidence + time-resolution + shortest-note range sliders (labelled, with <output> live values), Trace/Read/Transcribe buttons, and the live tuner (mic + sample-stream fallback). Confidence changes re-render the curve/pitchgram/histogram client-side without re-running inference. Manual keyboard/focus and slider tap-target verdict on real touch hardware still pending.
rubric.notes: auto-checkable parts pass; slider tap-target/focus verdict is manual-evidenced.

rubric.evidence: The verdict, pitchgram, note-grid curve, note histogram, tuner needle, transcribed note sequence, and the multi-model transcript+contour are all derived from the real per-frame salience returned by onnxruntime-web (and Whisper for words). Below the confidence threshold the note blanks and the tuner needle rests — honest, never a made-up pitch.

Buttons have accessible names; the pitchgram/curve canvases carry descriptive aria-labels and a text alternative (the note-histogram table with <caption> + scope); status is a role=status aria-live region; range sliders are <label for> associated with visible <output> values; the tuner meter exposes an aria-label updated live. Guidance: canvas content exposed via aria-label + data-table alternative rather than the non-Baseline HTML-in-canvas (requestPaint) API — recorded exception. Conformance buttons-have-names / live-status-region auto-checks pass.
rubric.notes: Full SR pass on real hardware pending an agent read.

rubric.evidence: Responsive matrix (automated): desktop 1280×800 and mobile 360×740 DPR3 — desktop clean · mobile clean (no horizontal overflow, no clipped controls). Layout uses flex-wrap + a min(100%,220px) auto-fit param grid with min-inline-size:0, and canvases are inline-size:100% (css-layout guidance). Read the mobile screenshot: legible, no overflow, adequate tap targets on links/chips. Screens: reports/responsive/screens/pitch-detection/{desktop,mobile}.png.
rubric.notes: auto-checkable overflow/clipping pass; slider-thumb tap-target legibility verdict is manual-evidenced.

rubric.evidence: All inference is off the main thread (onnxruntime-web in a Web Worker), batched at 128 frames/run to bound each task; the live-tuner path drops frames while the worker is busy (inFlight guard) so INP stays low (identify-inp-causes guidance). The pitchgram is composited via an OffscreenCanvas at native resolution then scaled once. Scale-clip analyse latency 1225 ms for 240 frames; confidence-threshold changes are a light client-side re-render, no re-inference.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing (min(100%,220px) auto-fit tracks, min-inline-size:0) and inline-size:100% media instead of fixed widths so control panels and canvases never overflow narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the parameter grid, sample row, field rows and readouts wrap; range sliders + all canvases set inline-size:100%;min-inline-size:0. Responsive matrix confirms no horizontal overflow at 360px or 1280px.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep interactions responsive by moving heavy JavaScript off the interaction path.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the entire CREPE frame batch loop (and the live-tuner streaming path) runs in a Web Worker via onnxruntime-web; the main thread only draws. The live path drops frames while the worker is busy; confidence changes re-derive views client-side, not re-inference.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js runs onnxruntime-web off-thread; conformance inference-off-main-thread = pass; live tuner produced smooth successive scale readings

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state in a live region; associate every control with a visible label and expose its current value; expose canvas content to assistive tech.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — loader + page status use role=status aria-live; range sliders are <label for> associated with a live <output>; canvases carry descriptive aria-labels and a text alternative (the note-histogram table with caption/scope). Exception: used aria-label + data-table alternative rather than the non-Baseline HTML-in-canvas requestPaint API to expose the visualisation.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance buttons-have-names / live-status-region = pass; note table rendered with <caption> + scope=col

openQuestions: On real touch hardware, are the confidence / time-resolution / shortest-note slider thumbs comfortable to drag (automated scans tend to flag sub-24px range thumbs)?
Does the live tuner stay smooth on a low-end mobile CPU running CREPE per window in real time, and does the mic-permission rationale read clearly before the prompt?
CREPE occasionally jumps an octave on breathy speech (visible as spikes in the multi-model prosody contour) — is a light median/viterbi smoothing worth adding without hiding the raw model behaviour?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass for pitch-detection on a real touch device: confirm slider tap-targets + focus order + the live tuner under a real microphone, then re-affirm support=ok.
Consider an AudioWorklet upgrade for the live-tuner mic tap (currently ScriptProcessorNode) to cut audio-thread overhead on mobile.
Add optional pitch-contour smoothing (median filter / Viterbi) as a toggle to reduce octave jumps on speech, keeping the raw curve available.

summary: Revision-1 critique for pitch-detection (CREPE monophonic pitch-estimation via onnxruntime-web). Real in-browser inference confirmed end-to-end across all five pages (fresh Download → real f0 curve + salience pitchgram; basics→A4, live tuner scale readings, humming→note sequence, CREPE×Whisper transcript+prosody). Auto-checkable dimensions pass (14/14 conformance auto-assertions); slider tap-target/focus and live-tuner-on-mobile verdicts await an agent matrix read on real hardware.
